question: Will Jesus Christ return before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if The Second Coming of Jesus Christ occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will Jesus Christ return before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if The Second Coming of Jesus Christ occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's "Will Jesus Christ return before 2027?" market reflects near-unanimous skepticism at 96.5% implied probability for "No," driven by the stark absence of verifiable biblical signs—such as global cataclysms or widespread divine manifestations—despite persistent end-times buzz in pop culture and social media. Historical precedents of failed Second Coming predictions, from medieval prophecies to modern fringe claims like a debunked April 2026 viral post, reinforce this positioning, with early 2026 memes briefly doubling low Yes odds before rational capital restored dominance. As the December 31, 2026, deadline nears, upset scenarios remain slim: a universally acknowledged messianic figure or apocalyptic event reshaping global sentiment, though traders price such black swans as negligible amid cultural desensitization to doomsday narratives.

question: Will Oprah Winfrey win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination on Polymarket, reflecting his national profile built through aggressive media challenges to Republican policies, robust fundraising, and executive experience as California governor ahead of his term ending in 2027. Recent appearances by Kamala Harris, Josh Shapiro, and Andy Beshear at the April National Action Network convention underscored the wide-open primary field, with polls like Echelon Insights showing Harris slightly ahead at 22% to Newsom's 21%, though markets prioritize electability over surveys. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws progressive support via youth and base mobilization, while Jon Ossoff benefits from Georgia's battleground success. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results in swing states, DNC endorsements, and fundraising tallies differentiating viable paths to delegates.

question: Will LeBron James win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

events.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
events.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election at 21.6% implied probability, buoyed by his role as heir apparent to President Trump and recent head-to-head polling like UMass Lowell's late-March survey showing him at 33% over Gavin Newsom's 30%. California Governor Newsom trails closely at 17.3%, capitalizing on his national profile as a leading Democratic critic of the administration, while Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 10.7% gains from high-profile foreign policy moves including Venezuela operations and Iran negotiations. The fragmented field across both parties keeps the race tight pre-primaries, with 2026 midterms poised to deliver momentum through congressional control, turnout in battlegrounds, and potential endorsements that could consolidate odds.
